design_philosophy.core_loop: Progress through urban stages defeating waves of enemies and bosses using melee combat, with resource management through health pickups and scoring for extra lives/credits
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Crowd control and positioning against multiple simultaneous enemies while managing limited health and resources
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as a tough vigilante (Haggar or Cody) cleaning up Metro City streets through direct combat against criminal gangs
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: SNES port makes deliberate cuts from arcade original (removal of Guy character, Industrial Area stage, two-player co-op) due to ROM constraints, creating a streamlined single-player experience that prioritizes arcade-style challenge over feature parity

difficulty.lives_system: Standard lives system with extra lives obtainable through high scores (50,001+ points per life) and secret Bay Area dog bonuses (1 credit every 60 seconds from 2nd dog, 1 life every 60 seconds from 3rd dog)
difficulty.continues_system: Credit-based system; standard game starts with 3 credits, can be increased to 5 or 9 through high score table manipulation (CAP/COM/YLM or CAP/COM/GUY initials)
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression with no mid-stage checkpoints; death results in life loss with continue available if credits remain
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Game includes hidden difficulty scaling system with DifficultyA (increases over time up to $17-$1A based on DifficultyB setting) and DifficultyB (four selectable levels from low to high, affecting difficulty frame counter rollover from 10 seconds to 5 seconds); debug mode accessible via cheat codes allows 9x slow motion for skill accommodation

progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage progression is linear through sequential areas; unused hidden level select present in code (accessible via cheat code) but removed from final menus
replayability_hooks: High score chase with secret bonus conditions (Bay Area dog positioning mechanics require pixel-precise coordination)
Two difficulty modes available in Options
Hidden debug features suggest development testing replayability
Multiple playable characters (Haggar vs Cody) with different movesets

world_building_techniques: Stage environment design (Bay Area stage with background dogs, storeroom setting)
Enemy character types indicating gang presence
Metro City setting tied to Street Fighter universe
Mad Gear Gang as overarching antagonist organization

boss_design.philosophy: Pattern-based encounters designed to test player's mastery of movement and attack timing against iconic character archetypes; boss variety signals progression through different criminal organization levels

faction_indicators: Mad Gear Gang as primary criminal faction
Gang member enemy types indicate organizational hierarchy
Boss characters represent command structure (Sodom, Rolento, Andore as named lieutenants)
danger_signals: Crowd encounters signal gang territory control
Progressive stage difficulty indicates approaching leadership
Named boss appearances signal confrontation with gang hierarchy
environmental_storytelling: Bay Area urban stage setting establishes Metro City geography
Storeroom stage suggests gang operational infrastructure
Stage progression through criminal-controlled zones (implied through layout and enemy placement)
Metro City backdrop ties narrative to broader Street Fighter universe conflict
